records.ety: [OE. clathen, clothen, clethen, AS. clāðian, clǣðan. See Cloth.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Clothed (klōd) or Clad (kld); p. pr. & vb. n. Clothing.]

defs: To put garments on; to cover with clothing; to dress.
quotes: Go with me, to clothe you as becomes you.

defs: To provide with clothes; as, to feed and clothe a family; to clothe one's self extravagantly.
quotes: Drowsiness shall clothe a man with rags.
The naked every day he clad, When he put on his clothes.

defs: Fig.: To cover or invest, as with a garment; as, to clothe one with authority or power.
quotes: Language in which they can clothe their thoughts.
His sides are clothed with waving wood.
Thus Belial, with with words clothed in reason's garb.
Care no more to clothe eat.
